phase_name: Session 38: Hodge star identification — the missing operator
phase_dir: N/A - research sessions, not GSD phase

completed_tasks.name: Tautology killed — Q_W>=0 requires zeros on critical line
Digamma asymptotic: wr_diag[n] = C - log(n) + O(1/n), coeff exactly -1
Prime near-neutrality: per-prime trace O(w(pk)), total << analytic trace
Spectral gap: Gershgorin fails by 10^8x, Weyl by 5x, no conversion works
SOS Neg>=Ma+Pos holds but is equivalent to RH, not independent
Lefschetz wall: [W02,X]|null = 0 for all X, commutators dead on null block
Hodge star identified as the missing operator for sign determination

remaining_tasks.name: Construct the discrete Hodge star on null(W02)
Identify H^{1,0} and H^{0,1} decomposition of null(W02)
Test if the Hodge star gives sign determination from representation theory
Connect to Connes-Consani scaling site Hodge structure

blockers.description: The ample class W02 = 0 on null(W02), so all Lefschetz commutators vanish there. The sign must come from a DIFFERENT geometric operator — the Hodge star.
blockers.workaround: Construct the Hodge star from the explicit formula structure, not from W02.

blockers.description: Q_W >= 0 at finite lambda is NOT unconditional — requires zeros on critical line. The strong convergence argument is circular.
blockers.workaround: Need a non-spectral proof of positivity, e.g., via Hodge star decomposition.

decisions.decision: The 'tautology' claim from Session 36 is WRONG
decisions.rationale: Fake off-line zero at Re=0.6 flips Q_W to -0.085. Q_W>=0 encodes actual arithmetic (zero locations), not just the explicit formula identity.

decisions.decision: Digamma diagonal is the main driver of negativity
decisions.rationale: wr_diag[n] = C - log(n) with coeff exactly -1. Analytic trace -42 to -65 dwarfs prime margin of 3-10. Primes are nearly transparent.

decisions.decision: The Hodge star is the missing operator
decisions.rationale: Lefschetz (W02) vanishes on null — can't constrain signs via commutators. The Hodge star decomposes null into H^{1,0}+H^{0,1}, giving signs from representation theory. Not yet constructed for scaling site.
dim ~ log(lambda), zeros ~ lambda. Ratio grows as exp(dim/24). The matrix is an exponentially compressed arithmetic certificate. This parallels Deligne's proof structure.

next_action: Construct the discrete Hodge star operator on null(W02). The Hodge star in classical geometry comes from the complex structure (it rotates H^{1,0} to H^{0,1}). In our setting, the complex structure might come from: (1) the functional equation s <-> 1-s, (2) the Hilbert transform, (3) the scaling operator's spectral decomposition, or (4) the explicit formula's analytic structure. Test each candidate.
context_notes: Session 38 was the deepest analytical session yet. Killed the tautology claim (Q_W>=0 requires RH, not unconditional). Proved digamma asymptotic (wr_diag = C - log(n), coeff -1). Proved prime near-neutrality (trace O(sqrt(lambda)) << analytic trace O(N*log(N))). Showed all standard spectral bounds fail by 10^8x. Mapped the Lefschetz wall: W02=0 on null makes all commutators vanish. Identified the Hodge star as the missing operator — it decomposes null(W02) into H^{1,0}+H^{0,1} and gives eigenvalue signs from representation theory. The parity decomposition (even/odd) is clean (23+22) but is NOT the Hodge decomposition (it doesn't determine signs). The true Hodge star must come from the arithmetic structure, not just symmetry. Key files: session38_*.py.
